summary: Matched the small fighter common file `src/melee/ft/ft_0BF0.c`, replacing three assembly functions from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` with C: `ftCo_800BF034`, `ftCo_800BF108`, and `ftCo_800BF228`. The matched code handles motion-state changes for ice-camera-hit/dead-up-fall and sleep states, sets fighter bitfields, conditionally spawns held/attached items for Fox, Ness, and Dr. Mario, and exposes a boolean helper that checks whether a fighter is currently in either of the two matched motion states. The PR also added the new object file to `obj_files.mk`, created a dedicated `ft_0BF0.h`, moved the three declarations out of `ft_0BEC.h`, and replaced placeholder header usage in `ft_0BEF.h` with `ft/forward.h`.
what_changed: Removed the assembly implementations of `ftCo_800BF034`, `ftCo_800BF108`, and `ftCo_800BF228` from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`.
Added `src/melee/ft/ft_0BF0.c` containing matched C implementations for the three functions. The PR report shows the new file at 100.00% with zero score across all three functions.
`ftCo_800BF034` now calls `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` with `ftCo_MS_DeadUpFallHitCameraIce`, sets `fp->x2219_b2` and `fp->x2219_b1`, and spawns character-specific items for Fox and Ness.
`ftCo_800BF108` performs the same bitfield/item setup but changes to `ftCo_MS_Sleep` and also handles Dr. Mario by calling `ftMr_SpecialN_VitaminRandom` and `it_802C09C4`.
`ftCo_800BF228` was typed as `bool ftCo_800BF228(ftCo_GObj* gobj)` and checks for a non-null gobj/fighter before returning true only for `ftCo_MS_DeadUpFallHitCameraIce` or `ftCo_MS_Sleep`.
Created `src/melee/ft/ft_0BF0.h` with typed declarations for the three matched functions.
Removed the `ftCo_800BF034`, `ftCo_800BF108`, and old `UNK_RET ftCo_800BF228(UNK_PARAMS)` declarations from `src/melee/ft/ft_0BEC.h`.
Added `$(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/ft_0BF0.c.o` to `TEXT_O_FILES` in `obj_files.mk` before the remaining `ft_08A4.s.o` assembly object.
Included `ft/ft_0BF0.h` from `src/melee/ft/ftdemo.c` so users of the moved declarations compile against the new header.
smart_moves: Split the newly matched functions into a dedicated `ft_0BF0.c`/`.h` pair instead of leaving declarations in neighboring `ft_0BEC.h`, improving address-range locality and making the decomp slice easier to search.
Improved function typing while matching: `ftCo_800BF228` was upgraded from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` to a concrete `bool` return and `Fighter_GObj`/`ftCo_GObj` parameter style.

Used existing fighter struct fields and enums (`fp->kind`, `fp->motion_id`, `ftCo_MS_DeadUpFallHitCameraIce`, `ftCo_MS_Sleep`, `FtPart_RThumbNb`) rather than raw offsets and magic motion IDs, while preserving observed behavior.
Modeled the assembly switch on fighter kind as explicit `switch (fp->kind)` cases for Fox, Ness, and Dr. Mario, making item-spawn side effects visible and auditable.
Kept a small `MUST_MATCH` unused stack workaround in the two larger functions, documenting that it exists solely for matching rather than gameplay logic.
Removed the local `.sdata2` float constants from assembly as the C calls encode the same `0, 1, 0` float arguments to `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`.
decomp_lessons: When replacing a small assembly island with C, add the new C object immediately before the remaining assembly object in `obj_files.mk` to preserve link order.
Declarations should migrate with the address-range file they belong to; do not leave newly matched `0BF0` symbols in an older neighboring header like `ft_0BEC.h` when a dedicated header exists.
A boolean helper that reads like a motion-state predicate can often be given a real `bool` signature once null checks and motion IDs are understood.
Bitfield writes from `rlwimi` can map cleanly to named boolean bitfields such as `fp->x2219_b2 = true` and `fp->x2219_b1 = true` when the struct already exposes those fields.

Character-kind branches in common fighter code may call into character-specific item modules; include the relevant type and item headers only as needed (`ftFox/types.h`, `itfoxblaster.h`, `itnessbat.h`, `itdrmariopill.h`).
Small stack artifacts may need `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` dummy locals; keep them visibly marked as matching-only with comments.
naming_conventions: New file and header names follow the address-range convention: `ft_0BF0.c` and `ft_0BF0.h` for functions beginning at `0BF034`.
Common fighter functions retain the `ftCo_` prefix and address-based names: `ftCo_800BF034`, `ftCo_800BF108`, `ftCo_800BF228`.
Header guard follows the existing GALE01 address style: `GALE01_0BF034`.
Fighter kind enum names use `FTKIND_*`, e.g. `FTKIND_FOX`, `FTKIND_NESS`, and `FTKIND_DRMARIO`.
Motion-state enum names use `ftCo_MS_*`, e.g. `ftCo_MS_DeadUpFallHitCameraIce` and `ftCo_MS_Sleep`.
Fighter part enum names use `FtPart_*`, shown by `FtPart_RThumbNb`.
Fox data attributes use the typed `ftFox_DatAttrs` struct and existing field name `x20_FOX_BLASTER_GUN_ITKIND`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced three assembly functions totaling 556 bytes of matched C output while leaving the rest of `ft_08A4.s` intact.
Matched the assembly's `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` calls by passing motion IDs `0x0A`/`0x0B` as named enums and the float argument pattern equivalent to `0, 1, 0`.
Translated `rlwimi` byte-bit updates at fighter offset `0x2219` into two named bitfield assignments on `Fighter`.
Translated assembly comparisons on `fp->kind` values `1`, `8`, and `0x15` into switch cases for Fox, Ness, and Dr. Mario.
Preserved item return storage by assigning the return values of `it_802AE994`, `it_802AD590`, and `it_802C09C4` to `fp->item_gobj`.
Used `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` with an unused 8-byte stack array to satisfy stack layout requirements in `ftCo_800BF034` and `ftCo_800BF108`.
Converted the assembly null-check chain in `ftCo_800BF228` into nested C null checks before inspecting `fp->motion_id`.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type `ftCo_800BF034`, `ftCo_800BF108`, or `ftCo_800BF228`.
Look for later cleanups of the `MUST_MATCH` unused stack workaround in `ft_0BF0.c`.
Search for other PRs that move declarations out of neighboring address-range headers into newly matched headers.
Investigate whether `x2219_b1` and `x2219_b2` receive more descriptive names in later fighter struct work.
Search for item-spawn helper decompilation involving `it_802AE994`, `it_802AD590`, and `it_802C09C4`.
